info.title: Tabularium API
info.description: Tabularium — index, submit, and discover plugins.

tags.name: Submit
tags.description: Register a new plugin via OAuth.

get.description: Liveness/readiness probe. Returns `{ ok: true }` (200) when DB + cache + storage are reachable, `{ ok: false }` (503) otherwise. Intentionally terse to avoid leaking driver / setup-mode details to unauthenticated callers — admins should hit `/api/admin/diagnostics` for the full breakdown.

Paginated catalog. Supports `search` (name/description/slug), `category` (exact), `tag` (exact), `featured=1`, and `sort=updated|new|name|featured`. Returns facets so the UI can render filter chips without an extra request. Public — no auth required.

Full plugin record including release history and the rendered README HTML (sanitized via DOMPurify, cached 10 min). Public — no auth required. Pass `?locale=` to request a localized README; falls back to base language, then `en`, then the first available.

delete.description: Removes the plugin and all of its releases from the registry. Requires auth and only the owner can perform it. The upstream repo and the webhook on the repo are left untouched.

get.summary: Aggregated download counts per version + platform

post.description: Pulls `.tabularium` (and the README) from the repo at the given ref (defaults to the latest released tag or HEAD). Authenticated plugin owners only; rate-limited to 1/min per plugin.

get.summary: Resolve latest release for a platform
get.description: Returns the matching asset for the requested platform. `os` and `arch` are optional — if missing, the User-Agent is inspected; if still unknown, falls back to `universal`. The full per-platform map is always included so clients can present alternatives.

schema.description: Pass `1` to 302-redirect to the download URL instead of returning JSON.

post.description: Owner-only. Creates a pending transfer the recipient must accept (or reject) within 7 days. Cancel anytime via `DELETE /auth/me/transfers/:id`. Only one pending transfer per plugin at a time.

get.summary: Provider stats (stars / forks / last push) for a plugin
get.description: Lazily fetched from the upstream provider and cached for 10 minutes. Returns null fields when the provider is unreachable or the repo is private.

post.description: Re-fetches every asset of the given (or latest) release and updates `sha256` + `size`. Useful when the original webhook fetch raced asset upload. Set `force: true` to re-hash assets that already have a sha256. Authenticated plugin owners only; rate-limited to 1/min per plugin.

get.summary: Asset integrity (sha256 + size) for a specific release
get.description: Lookup endpoint for `sum.<host>`-style verifiers. When `release_assets` rows are present (post-ingest) the response includes the signed `jws` plus per-asset metadata (`name`, `sha256`, `size`, `attestation_bundle`). For legacy releases without per-asset rows yet, falls back to the platform-keyed JSON blob with `url`/`size`/`sha256`.
Browse the community wishlist of plugins users would like to see. Sorted by upvotes by default. Public — no auth required.

post.description: Add a new entry to the community wishlist. Requires auth. Slug must be unique across requests.

slug.description: URL-safe slug. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens.
URL-safe slug. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens.
URL-safe slug. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens.

post.description: Toggles the current user's vote on the request: if not yet voted, adds a vote; if already voted, removes it. Returns the new total and the user's new vote state. Requires auth.

post.summary: Toggle a claim on a plugin request
post.description: Adds or removes the current user's claim on the request. Claims are non-exclusive — multiple users can claim the same request. They do not block plugin submission.

get.description: Returns a structured PluginDocs document with the current core schema, global extensions, per-kind extensions, examples (YAML + JSON), and links to the OpenAPI spec. Generated on every request from the live registry state. Pass ?format=md for a single flat markdown blob (LLM-friendly).

Returns the registry-wide list of plugin kinds defined by the admin. Plugin authors mark a plugin's kind by adding the matching value to their `tags` field. Public — no auth required. A kind may opt in to a dedicated public catalogue page at `/c/:key` via `publicPageEnabled`; consumers treat the field as falsy when absent.

patch.description: Partial update. `status` toggles approval. `featured: true` pins to the landing grid (use `featuredOrder` to set position). `category`/`tags` are normally driven by the `.tabularium` manifest but can be overridden here.

post.summary: Manually replay the latest release ingest
post.description: Re-fetches the upstream latest release via the owner's stored OAuth token and runs the same ingest path the webhook uses. Useful when the webhook never fired (initial setup) or upstream timed out. Skips signature verification.

post.summary: Re-fetch the .tabularium manifest from the repo
post.description: Pulls `.tabularium` (and the README) from the repo at the given ref (defaults to the latest released tag or HEAD).
Re-fetches every asset of the given (or latest) release and updates `sha256` + `size`. Useful when the original webhook fetch failed. Set `force: true` to re-hash assets that already have a sha256.
Apply the same action to up to 100 plugins in one round-trip. `delete` cascades to releases. `reject` accepts an optional reason. Single audit-log entry per call.

get.summary: List all markdown pages (grouped by slug)

post.description: Creates the default-locale row unless `locale` is given. Pass `path` to mount at any non-reserved URL.

post.summary: Render markdown to sanitized HTML (admin live preview)

patch.summary: Update a markdown page (creates the translation row if absent)

delete.summary: Delete a markdown page (all locales) or one translation when `locale` is given

get.summary: List existing translations for a page

post.summary: Mark the setup wizard as completed
post.description: Sets `setup.completed_at` in settings. Frontend stops redirecting to /welcome after this.

post.summary: Probe the OAuth authorize endpoint for a provider instance
post.description: Issues a GET (manual redirect, 5s timeout) against the provider-kind specific authorize URL. A 302/400 typically means "configured correctly". 5xx or fetch errors are reported as not-ok. Records an audit entry with the outcome.

post.summary: Upload a logo for a provider instance
post.description: Multipart upload. Field name must be `file`. Accepts png/jpg/webp/svg up to 512 KB. Storage backend is set in `/admin/infra/storage`. The instance row is updated to reference the new URL (includes a cache-buster query param).
Flip `enabled` on up to 100 provider instances in one round-trip. Unknown ids are returned in `missing` and silently skipped. Single audit-log entry per call.

put.description: Keys ending in `.client_secret`, `.password`, or `.token` are stored encrypted automatically.

get.description: Admin-only deep health/diagnostics. Returns driver names, mode, uptime, and per-check failure detail. The public `/healthz` returns only `{ ok }` to avoid leaking infrastructure details.

put.description: Toggle approval mode, adjust per-bucket rate limits, and configure the manifest filename + schema URL. Pass `reset: true` to clear a rate-limit override; pass `null` or an empty string for the manifest fields to revert to defaults.

put.summary: Replace the full list of app URL schemes
put.description: Overwrites the stored app_url_schemes setting with the provided list. Each scheme needs a name + URL scheme (lowercase, starts with a letter). Optional `kinds` filter restricts the scheme to specific plugin kinds.

get.summary: Get current + previous registry signing keys

post.description: Generates a new Ed25519 keypair, demotes the current key to .previous (overwriting any earlier previous), and records an audit entry.

get.summary: Get current + default branding (with translation maps)

put.description: Partial update. `tagline` and `footerText` set the default-locale value; `taglineTranslations` / `footerTextTranslations` set per-locale overrides (pass null/empty to clear).

post.description: Multipart upload. Field name must be `file`. Accepts png/jpg/webp/svg up to 512 KB. Storage backend is set in `/admin/infra/storage`. The branding `logo_url` setting is updated to reference the new URL (includes a cache-buster query param).

Multipart upload. Field name must be `file`. Accepts png/jpg/webp/svg/ico up to 256 KB. Storage backend is set in `/admin/infra/storage`. The branding `favicon_url` setting is updated to reference the new URL (includes a cache-buster query param).

put.description: Pass an empty string for any s3 field to clear it. Access / secret keys are stored encrypted. `disk` writes under `data/uploads/` and serves via `/uploads/`. `s3` uses `Bun.S3Client` (compatible with AWS, MinIO, R2).
Persists the driver choice (and optional redisUrl) and immediately reconfigures the running cache. redisUrl is stored encrypted. Pass empty string to clear the override.
Replaces the entire home_copy blob. Empty strings clear the override for that locale.

get.summary: List the caller’s admin API tokens

post.summary: Create a long-lived admin API token (M2M)
post.description: Returns the plaintext token once. Store it immediately — the registry only persists a sha256 hash and cannot show the token again. Use it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` against any /api/admin/* endpoint.

get.summary: Read the current manifest extensions delta + merged schema

put.description: The delta is a flat record of `{ propertyName: JSONSchemaNode }`. The registry wraps it into an object schema and merges it with the locked core. Pass `null` or `{}` to clear.

get.summary: Read email-recovery state for the current admin

put.description: Toggle whether rootCredentials survives the first OAuth link, and/or rotate the bootstrap email + password for the calling admin.

delete.summary: Delete the rootCredentials row for the current admin

get.summary: Resolve a published page by its public path
get.description: Returns sanitized HTML in the requested locale, with fallback to the default locale.
Returns sanitized HTML for a page in the requested locale, falling back to the default locale if missing.

post.summary: Probe a database URL during install
post.description: Tries to connect to the given URL and run SELECT 1. Reports the detected dialect either way. Bootstrap-only.

get.summary: Check whether the registry needs first-time setup
Env values to prefill the install wizard

post.summary: Finalize install: connect DB, migrate, promote bootstrap admin, exit
Bootstrap sign-in for the install wizard

get.summary: Public instance info (app URL schemes, etc)
get.description: Public read-only endpoint. Clients (frontend, third-party browsers) consume this on boot to learn which desktop apps can handle "Open in App" deep-links for plugin installation.

post.summary: Publish a release from a CI / CLI using a publisher token
post.description: Token-authenticated counterpart to the webhook ingest path. First push of an unknown slug auto-claims it (requires publish:* scope and a repoUrl). Subsequent pushes update the plugin's latest release. Manifest is validated; asset URLs are hashed in the background.
GitHub / GitLab / Gitea (and Forgejo) post releases here. Signature scheme is picked per plugin's linked provider instance kind.

get.description: Public read-only endpoint. Pass `?locale=` to fetch the tagline/footer in a specific language (falls back to default locale).

get.summary: List submittable repos across linked identities

post.summary: Dry-run a submission: fetch + validate the manifest, no writes
post.description: Resolves the latest release + .tabularium for the given repo and returns a preview the frontend can render before the user confirms. No database writes. Useful so the submit page can show name/description/kind/screenshots from the manifest instead of asking the user to type them again.

get.description: Public read-only endpoint. Returns the admin-configured eyebrow + feature-section overrides. Empty translation maps mean "fall back to the built-in copy".
Public reference for plugin authors. Returns the TypeBox JSON Schema, an example, and the registry's active kind keys.

post.summary: Validate a manifest body against the registry schema
post.description: Public validator for authoring tools / CI. Returns 200 + ok:false on validation failure so HTTP-status-branching tools always reach the body. 4xx is reserved for malformed requests and oversized bodies.

Returns the plaintext token once. Store it immediately — the registry only persists a sha256 hash and cannot show the token again. Use it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` against POST /api/publish/:slug.
Idempotent — flips the server-side session record to revoked and clears the auth cookie. Returns `{ ok: true }` regardless of prior auth state.

get.summary: Get current user with linked identities

delete.description: Cascade-deletes identities + root credentials and clears the session cookie. Refuses with 409 if you still own plugins — transfer or delete them first.

get.summary: List plugin transfers involving the current user
get.description: Returns both incoming (you are the recipient) and outgoing (you initiated) transfers. Each row carries plugin + actor names already joined in.

post.summary: Respond to a transfer (accept / reject / cancel)
post.description: Recipient: `action=accept` (moves ownership) or `action=reject`. Sender: `action=cancel`. Idempotent — once terminal, returns 409.

get.summary: Start OAuth flow for a provider instance
get.description: Redirects to the provider instance's authorize page. Instance IDs are configured by the admin (e.g. `github`, `gitlab`, `forgejo-acme`). Set `link=1` to attach this identity to the currently signed-in user.

get.summary: OAuth callback for a provider instance

post.description: Allowed only while no admin exists. The first registered account becomes admin. Credentials are stored in `root_credentials` and deleted automatically the first time the admin links an OAuth identity.
Recovery path for the initial admin. Closes permanently once the admin links any OAuth identity.
